# Discussion

The manubot paper [@doi:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1007128] established the
pattern; the integration with Quarto [@quarto2024] generalizes it.

Notice that both citations above appeared in earlier chapters too,
and that they resolve to the same entries here. The pre-render hook
runs once per render across the whole project, so each unique cite key
is resolved exactly once regardless of how many chapters cite it; the
resulting `references.json` is what pandoc citeproc reads for every
chapter.

Each chapter renders its own bibliography section at the bottom; that's
Quarto's default for HTML book output. A future quartobot CLI option
(`quartobot resolve --aggregate`) could optionally produce a single
references page; the example here uses Quarto's idiomatic per-chapter
layout.

For a working manuscript-shape demonstration of the same pattern, see
the [Venice 2026 hackathon manuscript](https://github.com/seandavi/2026-venice-spatial-hackathon-manuscript) —
a 25-author preprint running the CI / permalink / banner half of the
pattern.

For a book in production use, this example is intentionally minimal.
The pattern scales to longer works: more chapters, more citations, the
same configuration.
